column3: Chapter Description (Optional)
column4: Question ID (Mandatory and Unique, for example: 1.1, 1.2, Etc)
column5: Question Title (Mandatory)
column7: Answer Type (Mandatory, one of the following options: 1 for Open Answer, 2 for Dropdown - Single Select, 3 for Dropdown - Single Select - Open answer, 4 for Date, 5 for Dropdown - Multiple Select, 6 for Dropdown - Multiple Select - Open answer)
column8: Dropdown Options (Mandatory if column G is Dropdown. List options for the dropdown separated by a pipe character, for example: Yes|No|Undefined)
column9: DropDown Warnings (Optional, for every option on column H you can define a message, for example: Great|Oh|Think twice)
column10: Dropdown Scoring (Mandatory, set this to 1 if you want to enable scoring for this question. Otherwise set to 0)
column11: Dropdown Option Score (Mandatory if J is set to 1, set the score of the question as an integer positive number, for example 10)
column12: Dropdown Option Score Multiplier (Mandatory if J is set to 1. For every option of the dropdown in column H define an integer positive number that will be multiplied by the value on column K, for example: 1|2|10)
column13: Dropdown Option Show another Question (Optional and only possible if column G is dropdown.For every option in column H you can define one or more question IDs from column D to be shown when the option is selected. For example: 1.2 1.6||6.7 would display questions 1.2 and 1.6 if the first option is selected, none if the second is selected and 6.7 if the third option is selected)
column14: Question Comments & Attachments (Mandatory, select 0 if you do not want to show this option or 1 if you want to let users provide comments and attachments for each question)

column2: Standard - Definition and Allocation of Information Security Roles
column3: The following roles are defined within the ISMS and apply where relevant to the organization: - Chief Information Security Officer (CISO): Provides overall direction for information security, approves ISMS strategy, and ensures alignment with business objectives. - Information Security Manager: Operates and maintains the ISMS, coordinates risk management, monitoring, and audit activities, and ensures policies and controls are implemented. - Security Officer: Supports day-to-day security operations and assists with incident, vulnerability, and control monitoring activities. - Data Protection Officer (DPO): Oversees compliance with data protection and privacy requirements and acts as the contact point for personal data protection matters. - Internal Audit Department: Independently evaluates the effectiveness of information security controls, policies, and processes and reports findings to management. - Employees: Protect organizational information, comply with information security policies, and report security incidents or weaknesses. - Contractors: Comply with information security requirements applicable to their engagement and protect organizational information accessed during their work. ---
column5: Does this requirement reflect your company current practice: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO): Provides overall direction for information security, approves ISMS strategy, and ensures alignment with business objectives.

column3: The following roles are defined within the ISMS and apply where relevant to the organization: - Chief Information Security Officer (CISO): Provides overall direction for information security, approves ISMS strategy, and ensures alignment with business objectives. - Information Security Manager: Operates and maintains the ISMS, coordinates risk management, monitoring, and audit activities, and ensures policies and controls are implemented. - Security Officer: Supports day-to-day security operations and assists with incident, vulnerability, and control monitoring activities. - Data Protection Officer (DPO): Oversees compliance with data protection and privacy requirements and acts as the contact point for personal data protection matters. - Internal Audit Department: Independently evaluates the effectiveness of information security controls, policies, and processes and reports findings to management. - Employees: Protect organizational information, comply with information security policies, and report security incidents or weaknesses. - Contractors: Comply with information security requirements applicable to their engagement and protect organizational information accessed during their work. ---
